lenses.name: new-failure-outcome-propagation
lenses.reason: tab_gone new outcome on active_session_id/active_target_id; all callers propagate via ? or degrade via .ok()

lenses.name: non-destructive-recovery
lenses.reason: binding_snapshot returns None while gone; stale binding not overwritten

lenses.name: inverse-regression-surface
lenses.reason: index-0 default -> bound target; legacy unpinned path preserved and tested

lenses.name: boundary-pipeline-trace
lenses.reason: batch + MCP boundaries traced; batch drops code and pinTab

lenses.name: cancellation-timeout-hygiene
lenses.reason: no new unbounded waits; save() fsync bounded, no new async timeouts

lenses.name: docs-behavior-parity
lenses.reason: all surface-map files present in diff (output.rs, README, SKILL, docs, mcp.rs)

deterministic.reason: output.rs, README.md, skill-data SKILL+refs, docs/src/app, mcp.rs all in diff
connect gains pinTab via call_connect; tab switch/close descriptions updated

findings.claim: attachedToTarget auto-attach path still steals active tab and overwrites pin binding
findings.evidence: actions.rs:949 uses add_page (activates+binds) for attached_page_sessions; setAutoAttach waitForDebuggerOnStart makes attachedToTarget the primary path for new pages; no-steal fix only patched targetCreated at actions.rs:1082; test_add_page_activates_and_binds proves add_page steals
findings.layer: caller-sweep + manager unit; e2e trigger unverified (Chrome)

findings.claim: batch --json output omits the tab_gone code field
findings.evidence: main.rs:1800 run_batch builds json!{command,success,result,error} with no code key; single-command path serializes full Response (code present via serde). Source-layer definitive.

findings.claim: --pin-tab/--no-pin-tab not injected per-command in batch mode
findings.evidence: pinTab injection lives only in single-command flow main.rs:1162-1166; run_batch only calls attach_plugins_to_command + attach_restore_config_to_command; an already-running unpinned daemon is never pinned by a --pin-tab batch, and --no-pin-tab cannot disable in batch

findings.claim: pinning applied too late or binds index-0 not active tab
findings.evidence: execute_command sets state.pin_tab before dispatch; apply_tab_binding_on_attach runs inside auto_launch/handle_launch before the triggering action; auto-connect unbound path opens fresh tab (open_fresh_tab_for_auto_connect) so bound tab is agent-owned not foreign index-0; restore_target_binding selects the bound index. Traced at source; full attach ordering not e2e-forced (Chrome).

findings.claim: gone bound tab silently recovers instead of tab_gone under pin
findings.evidence: all active-page commands funnel through active_session_id()/active_target_id() which call check_bound(); tab_close(None) guarded; forced via test_restore_target_binding_missing_with_pin_enters_tab_gone + test_tab_close_current_in_tab_gone_state_errors (green). No unguarded active_page_index page-command path found in grep.

issueCandidates.claim: looks_like_target_id treats any >=16-char all-hex string as a target id; a user label that happens to be 16+ hex chars and is not a valid label resolves as Target, but is_valid_label gate mitigates
issueCandidates.whyOutOfScope: heuristic is new in this diff but no concrete collision demonstrated; low severity
